topic: Part 8b, Section 8b.3 eclipse-layers audit — post-ISS-1465 Chapman-language scope narrowing
change_summary: Decider self-apply patch for ISS-1465 (c6 H1 moderate): inserted 'Chapman-language' as a scope qualifier before 'fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below' in the parenthetical of the L3a sub-tests/goalpost/Fail-clause double-bind paragraph. Single-word insertion; no other content changed in the section. The c6-flagged E-PRED-B defender rejoinder is the target: by narrowing to Chapman-language, the geometric/directional sub-tests (E-PRED-A peak-tracks-geometry, E-PRED-B Hartland-beats-Ebro, E-PRED-C SG gravimeters, E-PRED-D non-path stations) are excluded from the inheritance claim, closing the defender's strongest single move against the parenthetical.
our_argument_summary: Under adoption of the dome author's fair-audit proposal, we restructure eclipse content into a four-layer stack (L1 ensemble anchor WIN-068, L2 Mohe timing WIN-011, L3a magnetic falsifier PRED-095, L3b gravity PRED-TIER-2, L4 optics caveat) and publish a codified magnetic Tier 3 threshold. Two structural critiques survive adoption: (i) an ensemble 'anchor' fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor (OTS-wrong-side pattern in physics form); (ii) a magnitude-only Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe because the arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment of the same observational corpus. The post-EXP-230 double-bind paragraph argues that the 'signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' Fail-clause (our codification) admits two readings and the Pass band fails under either; the ISS-1465 fix now narrows the inheritance claim from 'any fair-audit Fail-clause' to 'any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause,' foreclosing the E-PRED-B-style directional-test rejoinder while preserving the forced-disjunction conclusion.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome publishes per-station PRED-095 predictions with dual baselines (BOU 2017 −10.9 nT, W004 empirical −22.24 nT) × coverage × FSF, plus four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests (E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D). Our tracker records PRED-095 as a single amplitude-band claim ('−17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations'). Our codified Pass band with embedded 'signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' Fail-clause is an auditor-side steel-man on top of the dome's amplitude prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Mixed and unchanged by this patch. FSF from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry (dome-derived). Baselines BOU 2017 and W004 are INTERMAGNET globe-measured values (globe-parasitic). Coverage fractions are Besselian-element computations assuming a spherical Earth.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET magnetometer network (BOU, W004, HAD, EBR, etc.)
Van Camp et al. 2001 (via WIN-011 cite)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Per-station tolerances (±1-3 nT) implicit in the dome's E001-E009 table; no explicit Chapman-mechanism null-test criterion in PRED-095 as registered; no explicit rule for handling site-level heterogeneity within the W004 −17 to −21 nT cluster.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by the ISS-1465 patch. The parenthetical's factual premise ('PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json contains no Chapman-language Fail criterion at all') remains corroborated by raw-text/03-predictions.txt and data/predictions.json.

code_analysis_tags.note: SEC-* items don't carry WIN-style code_analysis_tags. The eclipse-layer audit inherits WIN-068.derives_from_dome:false and WIN-025.relabels_standard:true via explicit cross-refs. Those tags are accurate as cited and untouched by this patch.

holes_found.description: Residual scope-narrowness: 'any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause' is closer to correct than the pre-patch 'any fair-audit Fail-clause' but is still slightly broader than the actual ambiguity. The A/B split (baseline-Sq null test vs eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo response) is a property of the specific phrase 'signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone' — most acutely, the word 'alone' is what admits both readings. A pedantic defender could construct a Chapman-language Fail-clause that avoids the A/B ambiguity by not using 'Sq variation alone' phrasing — e.g., 'Fail = path-integrated eclipse-modulated ionospheric-conductivity decrease not detected at Ebro ionosonde during eclipse-maximum ±30 min window.' That formulation invokes Chapman mechanism but specifies 'eclipse-modulated' and a path-integrated observable, foreclosing both interpretations. Under that framing the parenthetical's 'any Chapman-language' would still be over-broad. This is a materially narrower defender move than the E-PRED-B rejoinder the patch closed, and requires the defender to construct an alternative Chapman-language Fail-clause rather than cite one the dome has published. c6 recommended either the Chapman-language scope (which the decider chose) or the more precise 'framed against the ionospheric Sq baseline' scope; the decider picked the coarser option. Severity is minor, not moderate, because (a) the main defender move is closed and (b) the residual over-scope requires an auditor-constructed counterexample, not a pre-existing one.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish for a future EXP: swap 'any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause' for 'any fair-audit Fail-clause framed against the ionospheric Sq baseline' (c6 stronger-argument wording) or 'any Sq-variation-language fair-audit Fail-clause.' Not blocking — ISS-1465 is materially closed.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c6 H2: 'appended to our Pass band' remains in the opening clause of the double-bind paragraph, still semantically imprecise. The upstream threshold-codification paragraph quotes the Fail-clause as part of the 'our Pass band' definition — it was included in the codification from the outset, not bolted on afterward. A precision-minded defender could use 'appended' to argue the Fail-clause is a later addition and thus severable from the Pass band proper. The attribution-owning intent is preserved by the 'our codified Fail-clause ... that we appended' phrasing, so the rhetorical force is largely intact. ISS-1465 did not touch this; tracking as carry-forward.
holes_found.recommendation: No action from this review. Future polish EXP could swap 'appended to our Pass band' for 'included in our Pass band' or 'embedded within our Pass band.' Not blocking.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c6 H3: 'Chapman-language Fail criterion' remains jargon that a non-specialist reader has to reverse-engineer, and the new 'any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause' inserted by this patch doubles down on the jargon. Upstream Chapman references are several paragraphs earlier and are not visually linked. The specialist audience for SEC-8b can parse it; a general reader cannot. ISS-1465 slightly increases the jargon density of this paragraph but does not introduce the issue. Severity remains minor, not moderate, because the section's audience is adversarial-review specialists, not the general public.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional readability polish: if a future EXP splits the paragraph or adds cross-links, consider an inline gloss on first use, e.g., 'Chapman-language (i.e., invoking the Chapman Sq-dynamo mechanism).' Not blocking.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c6 H4: citation-by-our-tracker brittleness. 'PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json' cites our tracker file, not the dome's authoritative predictions page. An adversarial defender could respond that absence-of-Chapman-language in our mirror is not evidence of absence in the dome's registration. Verified in c6 that raw-text/03-predictions.txt corroborates the claim; the factual content holds, only the citation is stylistically fragile. ISS-1465 did not touch this.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish: co-cite the dome's own predictions page alongside data/predictions.json. Not blocking.

holes_found.description:
Carry-forward from c6 H5: the L3a 'sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause double-bind' paragraph remains ~407 words in a single

(verified word count above; essentially unchanged from the c6 ~402 figure — the one-word 'Chapman-language' insertion adds 1 word net). c5/c6 explicitly flagged this paragraph-length as needing a structural split EXP. ISS-1465 correctly scoped that out. Tracking so it does not get lost.
holes_found.recommendation:
No action from this review; follow the plan in c5/c6 stronger_arguments — a separate structural EXP should split the paragraph at 'The dome also publishes four nominally model-discriminating sub-tests' so the sub-test enumeration, goalpost retort, monitor.py scope concession, and Fail-clause double-bind each own their own

.

holes_found.description: Carry-forward from c6 stronger_arguments[2]: the upstream threshold-codification paragraph still contains 'globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission.' 'By the dome author's own admission' presupposes the dome author agrees PRED-095 is globe-parasitic as a whole; OPEN-001 is specifically about WGS84-dependent coordinates being IN PROGRESS to be replaced, not a blanket concession on globe-parasitism. ISS-1465 did not touch this paragraph.
holes_found.recommendation: No action from this review; scope for a future EXP narrowing 'by the dome author's own admission' to 'the dome author's own OPEN-001 admission that dome-native coordinates are IN PROGRESS.' Not blocking.

stronger_arguments.description: The Chapman-language scope-narrowing is the correct direction but the most defender-resistant wording is tighter still: 'any Sq-variation-baseline fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below.' The A/B split derives specifically from the ambiguity in the phrase 'ionospheric Sq variation alone' — reading A takes Sq as a no-eclipse baseline; reading B takes Sq as an eclipse-modulated mechanism. Both readings require 'Sq variation' in the Fail-clause; a Chapman-language Fail-clause that targets height-integrated conductivity, plasma-diffusion currents, or eclipse-modulated E-region dynamo currents without using 'Sq variation' phrasing would not split. 'Sq-variation-baseline' captures the actual operative scope. The current 'Chapman-language' wording is correct-in-direction but invites a technical defender to construct an alternative Chapman-mechanism Fail-clause that does not reduce to Sq-variation language. Suggest flagging for a polish EXP if the paragraph is ever re-touched.
sources: Chapman S. (1933) — defines Sq-dynamo as daily baseline current system, distinguished in practice from eclipse-driven conductivity transients
Matsushita S. (1965) 'Solar quiet-day variations' — review of Sq variation as baseline ionospheric current, standard reference for Sq vs disturbed-day decomposition

stronger_arguments.description: A complementary reinforcement for the post-ISS-1465 text: the A/B reading ambiguity is itself a property of the null hypothesis being steel-manned (ionospheric Sq-dynamo response to eclipse), not a bug in our wording. A short preamble to the parenthetical — 'The A/B split is inherent to the standard Sq-dynamo eclipse-response framework (Chapman 1933, Matsushita 1965): ionospheric physicists routinely decompose eclipse signals into baseline-Sq null vs eclipse-modulated Chapman response, and published discriminator-criteria rarely commit explicitly to either' — would preemptively disarm the defender's 'this is just your wording' rejoinder. Currently the burden-shift sentence ('the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate') carries this implicitly; making it explicit costs ~25 words and closes a residual attack surface. Out of scope for ISS-1465; good candidate for a polish EXP if 8b.3 is revisited.
sources: Kim & Chang (2018) eclipse ensemble — uses Sq-variation subtraction as standard analysis step without committing to A or B reading

stronger_arguments.description: Independent observation (not triggered by ISS-1465 but surfaced during this review): the 'monitor.py KAPPA-squared produces ~−29 nT, outside the W004 −17 to −21 nT cluster' observation remains buried in the middle of the double-bind paragraph. It is the section's sharpest factual hit — a dome defender cannot dismiss it without either admitting a bug, decoupling monitor.py from the public prediction, or abandoning the W004 cluster. Under any future structural-split EXP, this observation deserves a dedicated sentence in the goalpost-scope subparagraph rather than being embedded in the broader 'formula-selection-among-coexisting-dome-formulas' clause. Preserved from c6 for continuity.
sources: raw-text/monitor.py (KAPPA 1.672 applied twice in BOU 2017 formula, per WIN-025 three-formula audit)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt (W004 empirical −22.24 nT baseline vs −17 to −21 nT cluster)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author's fair-auditor proposal to structure the eclipse claim as a layered stack (anchor → timing → falsifier → caveat) is genuinely good methodology. Layered claims force explicit statements about what would falsify each layer, and spelling out a Tier 3 threshold is due diligence. The dome's E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D sub-tests are genuinely model-discriminating at the level at which they operate — E-PRED-B (Hartland beats Ebro despite lower coverage) is a directional test that does not reduce to a Chapman-Sq-dynamo expectation. The ISS-1465 scope-narrowing explicitly acknowledges this: the sub-tests are excluded from the inheritance claim by restricting it to Chapman-language Fail-clauses.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The layered presentation and E-PRED-A-through-D sub-tests do not repair the specific structural flaws the section identifies: (1) an ensemble 'anchor' fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor — layering does not change that; (2) the magnitude-only W004 Pass band remains non-discriminating regardless of whether the sub-tests are evaluated separately; (3) PRED-095's dome-derivation path remains globe-parasitic via WIN-025-documented INTERMAGNET baselines and WGS84-dependent FSF coordinates. The sub-tests can pass or fail without affecting the section's core argument about the magnitude-only W004 cluster; they are evaluated separately in Section 4.2 and Section 9. The ISS-1465 patch formally recognizes this scope separation by limiting the inheritance claim to Chapman-language Fail-clauses.

deeper_analysis:
The ISS-1465 patch is a minimal-surgery one-word insertion that lands cleanly. The decider inserted 'Chapman-language' as a scope qualifier before 'fair-audit Fail-clause' in the parenthetical 'so any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below.' Verified via git show 3ede96b that the diff to data/sections.json contains exactly this single-word change in part8b.html and no other edits to SEC-8b.3. The c6 moderate hole (ISS-1465) argued that the unscoped 'any' invited the E-PRED-B defender rejoinder: the dome's E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D sub-tests are explicitly model-discriminating, and a defender could construct a non-ambiguous Fail-clause around E-PRED-B (peak-over-peak Hartland > Ebro) that does not inherit the A/B reading ambiguity from 'ionospheric Sq variation alone.' The scope-narrowing to 'Chapman-language' excludes these directional/geometric sub-tests by construction: E-PRED-A (peak-tracks-geometry), E-PRED-B (Hartland-beats-Ebro coverage-defying directionality), E-PRED-C (SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal), and E-PRED-D (non-path stations below noise floor) are all geometric or amplitude-ratio tests that are not Chapman-language by any reasonable reading. The defender's main move on this parenthetical is now closed. Residual attack surface (minor): the scope-narrowing is still slightly broader than the actual ambiguity. The A/B split is a property of the specific phrase 'ionospheric Sq variation alone,' most acutely the word 'alone' — reading A treats 'alone' as 'no eclipse enhancement'; reading B treats 'alone' as 'only Sq-dynamo processes including eclipse-modulated ones.' A Chapman-language Fail-clause that does not use 'Sq variation alone' phrasing (e.g., a path-integrated ionospheric-conductivity test via ionosonde rather than ground-magnetometer amplitude) would not have the A/B split. A pedantic defender could argue 'any Chapman-language' is still over-scoped. But this requires the defender to construct an alternative Chapman-language Fail-clause, not cite one the dome has published — materially narrower than the E-PRED-B move. c6 recommended either the Chapman-language scope (taken) or the more precise 'framed against the ionospheric Sq baseline' scope; the decider picked the coarser-but-still-correct-in-direction option. This is a minor not moderate finding. Carry-forward minors from c6 (H2–H5 plus stronger_arguments[2]) are untouched and all remain: 'appended to our Pass band' imprecision; 'Chapman-language Fail criterion' jargon (now doubled in this paragraph by the ISS-1465 insertion, but still minor for the specialist audience); citation-by-our-tracker brittleness; paragraph-length (407 words in a single

); and 'globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission' over-attribution to OPEN-001. ISS-1465 correctly scoped these out; they are tracked here for continuity. Verdict robustness: the 'adopted-as-steel-man' section verdict continues to hold. The ISS-1465 patch strengthens rather than weakens the underlying argument — the forced-disjunction conclusion is preserved verbatim ('reading A surrenders discrimination to preserve consistency; reading B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible'), and the new scope-narrow is a defender-resistant refinement. Advocate mode: If I were the dome author reading SEC-8b.3 post-ISS-1465, my best remaining moves are (a) constructing an alternative Chapman-language Fail-clause that doesn't reduce to Sq-variation language (e.g., ionosonde path-integrated conductivity), forcing a second scope-narrow to 'Sq-variation-baseline'; (b) attacking the upstream 'globe-parasitic by construction, by the dome author's own admission' (c6 carry-forward) as overclaim; (c) attacking 'appended to our Pass band' as implying severability. None of these is the E-PRED-B move; all require more work than c6's best defender could have mustered. The section is materially stronger post-ISS-1465. Cross-WIN consistency check: ISS-1465 is localized to SEC-8b.3. The scope-qualifier pattern ('Chapman-language' as modifier) does not appear elsewhere that I can find in sections.json. WIN-068 and WIN-025 cross-refs are preserved by the patch. No propagation needed. Citation integrity: PRED-095 registration (2026-03-12, window closes 2026-08-12, per-station amplitude bands, four named sub-tests, BOU/W004 baselines, FSF from V12 H(r)/r) all verified in c6 and unchanged by this patch. The patched parenthetical's claim 'PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json contains no Chapman-language Fail criterion at all' remains corroborated by data/predictions.json (absence of fail_criteria/falsification fields with Chapman/Sq-dynamo language) and raw-text/03-predictions.txt (eclipse block contains no Chapman/Sq-dynamo null-test language). Fresh attack.'). queue_id set to 243, matches queue item. queue_pushed_at set to 2026-04-23T11:35:37.470Z, copied verbatim.
